compiled_state.title: As a DSO adopter, my GitHub identity and bridge environment are auto-configured
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance project-setup to present the authenticated GitHub user and offer a choice between using that identity or configuring a bot account, plus auto-generate BRIDGE_ENV_ID.
**Why**: Most users will use their own GitHub identity for bridge commits. The installer should make this the easy path while still supporting bot accounts. BRIDGE_ENV_ID has a deterministic formula and should never be prompted.
**Scope**:
- IN: gh api user to get authenticated identity; choice between own identity and bot account; BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN, BRIDGE_BOT_NAME derived from API response for own-identity path; BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL fallback chain (gh api user -> gh api user/emails -> prompt); bot account guided flow; BRIDGE_ENV_ID auto-generation from org/repo name; gh variable set for all identity values
- OUT: ACLI resolution (Story 2), Jira credentials (Story 3), gh fallback (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the installer presents the authenticated GitHub user info and asks whether to use it or configure a bot account
  Satisfies: "The installer presents the authenticated GitHub user and asks whether to use that identity or configure a separate bot account"
- When this story is complete, choosing own identity derives BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN and BRIDGE_BOT_NAME from gh api user response
  Satisfies: "BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN and BRIDGE_BOT_NAME are derived from the API response"
- When this story is complete, BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL is resolved via the fallback chain: gh api user email field, then gh api user/emails for primary verified email, then user prompt if both fail
  Satisfies: "the installer first checks the email field from gh api user; if null, it attempts gh api user/emails; if that also fails, it prompts the user"
- When this story is complete, choosing bot account guides the user through entering BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN, BRIDGE_BOT_NAME, and BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL
  Satisfies: "If they choose a bot, the installer guides them through all three values"
- When this story is complete, BRIDGE_ENV_ID is auto-generated as github-ORG-REPO without prompting
  Satisfies: "BRIDGE_ENV_ID is auto-generated deterministically from the GitHub org and repo name"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] gh api user email field is nullable — common when users have private email settings
- [Reliability] gh api user/emails requires user:email OAuth scope — not guaranteed in standard gh auth login token
- [Testing] Needs mock-compatible interface for gh API calls; test all three branches of email fallback chain
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: ADDED (adversarial review): [Reliability] This story calls gh api user and gh api user/emails for identity detection. If gh CLI is unavailable or unauthenticated, these calls will fail. Coordinate with Story d76e-33dd on the shared gh-availability check — either add a dependency or implement inline detection with manual fallback instructions.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks closed. Story complete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
